bullets.summary: Q2 FY2025 (ending July 28, 2024): revenue $30.0B (+122% YoY), data center revenue $26.3B (+154% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $0.68 (+152% YoY). Hopper demand described as strong going into H2 2024.
10-for-1 stock split completed June 10, 2024, lowering nominal share price from ~$1,200 to ~$120 to broaden retail access; stock had appreciated ~200% YTD at split date driven by AI chip demand.

Blackwell GPU architecture entered production in Q3 2024; CEO Jensen Huang stated demand is 'insane' and orders are 'booked out 12 months or so,' with Blackwell chips priced $30,000-$40,000 each and sold out well into 2025 per Wall Street analysts.

Q2 2024 (reported July 29, 2024): total revenue $85B (+14% YoY). Google Cloud crossed $10B quarterly revenue for the first time and exceeded $1B in operating profit. CEO Sundar Pichai attributed Cloud growth directly to AI adoption.

Google announced a quantum computing error-correction breakthrough in September 2024: a logical qubit composed of 105 physical qubits achieved 2.4x longer lifespan than its best constituent physical qubit, validating surface-code error correction as a practical path to fault tolerance.

Q4 FY2024 (ending June 30, 2024, reported July 30, 2024): revenue $64.73B (+15% YoY), EPS $2.95 vs $2.93 consensus. Azure grew 29% but missed the 31% estimate; stock fell ~7% after-hours on cloud growth shortfall, reflecting market's high sensitivity to AI-driven cloud expectations.

Azure AI customer base exceeded 60,000 organizations (+60% YoY) and GitHub Copilot was deployed in 77,000+ organizations. Microsoft's AI business described internally as fastest-growing in company history, on track to cross $10B annual revenue run rate.

Q2 2024 (reported August 8, 2024): record revenue $106M (+71% YoY). Space Systems revenue ~$79-84M; launch backlog topped 70 missions; total contract backlog reached $1.07B ($772M Space Systems, $294M launch). EPS -$0.08.

Neutron medium-lift rocket development: CFO acknowledged 2024 first-launch target was a 'green-light schedule' with zero margin for problems; Archimedes engine hot-fire testing had not yet begun. NSSLP Phase 3 Lane 1 eligibility a key motivator for the aggressive timeline.

Electron booked 31 missions in Q2 2024 across orbital and suborbital (HASTE) customers; successful launches for NASA, Synspective, Kineis, and KAIST. Year-to-date signed launches reached 17, with a multi-launch deal announced as the largest contract in company history at time of reporting.

BOTZ (Global X Robotics & AI ETF) as of mid-2024 held Keyence (9.37%), ABB (8.81%), Nvidia (8.28%), Fanuc (8.21%), and Intuitive Surgical (6.55%) as top five positions; IT sector 43.9%, industrials 37.9%. The ETF blends traditional industrial automation (Keyence, Fanuc, ABB) with AI-chip exposure (Nvidia) and surgical robotics (ISRG).

Industrial robotics sub-themes tracked by BOTZ include automation, unmanned vehicles/drones, non-industrial robots, and AI. Declining AI compute cost and rapid innovation in general-purpose robotics — particularly humanoids — cited as emerging catalysts alongside demographic and reshoring tailwinds.

Google published a quantum error-correction breakthrough (September 11, 2024): surface-code logical qubit built from 105 physical qubits achieved 2.4x longer lifespan than the best single physical qubit, validating error correction as a viable path to fault-tolerant quantum computing.

IBM completed its '100x100 Challenge' in 2024: running a 100-qubit circuit at depth 100 on the Heron processor, demonstrating utility-scale computation. Gate fidelities on Heron improved to 2Q error rates of ~8x10^-4, with circuits up to 5,000 two-qubit gates achievable.

QTUM ETF (Defiance Quantum) tracks 71 quantum computing and ML stocks in a modified equal-weight index; key holdings include IonQ (IONQ), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), and Rigetti Computing (RGTI). No commercial revenue from pure-play quantum vendors as of Q3 2024; wave is in research-and-development phase.

Constellation Energy announced a 20-year power purchase agreement with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island Unit 1 nuclear plant (835 MW, rebranded Crane Clean Energy Center), providing 24/7 carbon-free power to Microsoft AI data centers. CEG stock jumped ~22% on the day of announcement.

NUKZ ETF launched January 23, 2024; gained approximately 70% from launch through late September 2024. Holdings span the full nuclear ecosystem: uranium producers (Cameco), operating utilities (Constellation Energy), reactor manufacturers (NuScale), and advanced reactor developers.

Goldman Sachs forecast that AI data centers will consume 8% of total US electricity demand by 2030, up from 3% in 2024. Federal Energy Regulatory Commission projected US data center electricity demand to climb from 19 GW (2023) to 35 GW (2030), underpinning the nuclear-as-baseload narrative.
bullets.source: US Department of Energy / Common Fund

bullets.summary: Federal Reserve cut rates 50 basis points on September 18, 2024 — the first cut in over four years — moving the fed funds target to 4.75%-5.00%. An 11-1 vote; median FOMC participant penciled in 50 more bps of cuts in 2024 and 100 bps in 2025.

AGG yield-to-maturity stood at 4.20% as of September 9, 2024, above the Fed's long-run neutral rate estimate of 2.75%, suggesting the fund offered a positive real yield premium while the easing cycle was just beginning.

BIL (SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF) tracked 1-3 month Treasury bills; with the fed funds rate at 5.25%-5.50% through September 17, 2024, BIL was delivering near-5% annualized yield with minimal duration and credit risk — the highest cash-equivalent yield in over 15 years.

Federal Reserve's 50-basis-point cut on September 18, 2024 initiated the easing cycle; BIL's yield is mechanically linked to short-term rates, meaning the fund's income yield will decline as the cutting cycle proceeds through 2024-2025.

Gold prices were rising in 2024 driven by central bank demand (1,000+ tonnes purchased annually for three consecutive years through 2024) and anticipation of Federal Reserve rate cuts; IAU tracks spot gold and had been trending toward new all-time highs through Q3 2024.

The Fed's 50-basis-point cut on September 18, 2024 was viewed as broadly supportive of gold prices; historical Julius Baer analysis showed gold climbs 15%+ in the year following an initial rate cut coinciding with an economic slowdown.

VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) excludes many high-growth tech companies (AMZN, TSLA, GOOGL, NVDA absent from the index) due to its dividend-growth criteria, leading to structural underweight in AI/tech relative to broad large-cap benchmarks in a period of tech-driven market leadership.

With the Federal Reserve beginning its easing cycle in September 2024, dividend-growth equities in VIG (typically cash-flow-positive, quality-oriented companies) were expected to benefit from declining discount rates, which mechanically raises the present value of future dividend streams.

AI.rationale: Revenue is compounding at triple-digit rates across the major AI infrastructure plays. NVDA Q2 FY2025 data center revenue hit $26.3B (+154% YoY, 2024-08-28). GOOGL Cloud crossed $10B quarterly for the first time with $1B operating profit (2024-07-29). MSFT Azure AI customer base grew 60% YoY with 60,000+ customers (2024-07-30). Blackwell GPUs entered production with Jensen Huang describing demand as 'insane' and orders booked 12 months out. Adoption is real and compounding. However, the MSFT stock drop of ~7% on a 2-point Azure miss indicates stretched expectations, which is a cautionary sign. On balance, real revenue growth dominates and the wave reads surge, not yet peak.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: Rocket Lab (RKLB) reported Q2 2024 record revenue of $106M (+71% YoY, 2024-08-08) with a $1.07B total backlog and 70+ launches in the queue. Space Systems segment revenue is growing faster than launch. Electron is the global leader in small-launch. Commercial and government demand is compounding into a real backlog. Neutron remains in development — its delayed timeline is a risk but not a wave-cycle signal. The wave is in early-surge: real revenue, real customers, real backlog, but still pre-breakeven.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: The Constellation Energy / Microsoft Three Mile Island restart PPA (September 20, 2024) crystallized the AI-data-center-to-nuclear-power thesis with a real 20-year contract for 835 MW. CEG shares rose ~22% on the announcement. NUKZ ETF gained approximately 70% from its January 2024 launch through late September 2024. The fundamental driver — AI data center electricity demand growing from 19 GW to 35 GW by 2030 — is backed by FERC and Goldman Sachs projections available before 2024-10-01. Real contracts, real operating capacity, real demand driver = surge. Not yet peak because institutional nuclear capex commitments and regulatory restarts are still at the starting gun.

quantum.rationale: Research milestones are advancing — Google's error-correction paper (September 11, 2024) validated that logical qubits made of 105 physical qubits outperform physical qubits by 2.4x in lifespan, and IBM demonstrated utility-scale 100-qubit computations. However, no pure-play quantum vendor had commercial revenue as of Q3 2024. QTUM's holdings (IonQ, D-Wave, Rigetti) were pre-revenue or minimal-revenue. Progress visible in research data, not yet in stock-price revenue cycles. Classic buildup.

robotics.rationale: No date-confirmed (≤ 2024-09-30) bullet from this harvest provides a clear directional signal on the robotics wave specifically. BOTZ's holdings data lacked a confirmed pre-date performance figure. Per the grounding rule, insufficient evidence forces neutral.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG are not wave-aligned tickers. The Fed's 50-basis-point cut on September 18, 2024 is macro context, not a wave classification signal. Neutral by definition for general-markets tickers.
